response.justification: Candidate is an accomplished full-stack software engineer with strong web, backend, and some embedded/low-latency experience, but lacks the core requirements for a Principal Performance Architect role focused on AI accelerator hardware. There is no evidence of SoC/IP modeling, deep neural network optimization, GPU architecture work, SIMD/MIMD experience, or hardware-software co-design. Educational background (BS in CS) without advanced degree combined with absence of hardware/AI accelerator domain expertise makes this a poor fit.
strengths: Long track record (20+ years) of software engineering across multiple domains
Some low-latency systems experience at Sociomantic with async I/O and high-performance databases
Brief embedded systems exposure and C/C++ work at National Instruments
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, or AI accelerator hardware experience
No deep learning training/inference optimization or SIMD/MIMD background
Profile is web/full-stack focused, not hardware-software co-design or performance architecture

key_factors.factor: Missing AI accelerator / SoC hardware expertise
No deep learning optimization or GPU architecture background
Some C/C++ and low-latency systems exposure
